policy_statement: Annual monitoring and evaluation (M&E) reporting for NCCS implementation by ministries and compilation by CCCD/NCCC.
verbatim_text:
-  It  is  proposed  that  each  ministry,  on  an  annual  basis,  will  prepare  an  overview report covering where the ministry stands against achieving the specified goals and objectives, what was helpful and what are the main challenges encountered.
- ‡ It is proposed that the CCCD which acts as the technical secretariat of the NCCC will be responsible for collecting such annual reports from the ministries.
extraction_rationale: Contains a concrete proposed recurring reporting requirement and assigned institutional responsibility (CCCD).
reasoning: The text describes a proposed annual M&E reporting process: each ministry would prepare an annual overview report, and CCCD/NCCC would collect/compile these reports. This is a procedural commitment (reporting) with a frequency (“annual”), but it does not specify any quantifiable climate outcome target (e.g., emissions reduced, projects delivered) nor an explicit deadline/year by which an outcome must be achieved. The language is also explicitly non-binding (“it is proposed”), indicating weak commitment.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable target: No—verbatim text only mentions preparing an 'overview report' on progress and challenges, with no measurable outcome (e.g., % reductions, units, MW). Timeline: No—while it says 'on an annual basis' and 'annual reports', there is no explicit deadline year/date such as 'by 2030' (frequency is not a deadline). Binding mechanism: No—language is non-mandatory: 'It is proposed that each ministry... will prepare...' and 'It is proposed that the CCCD... will be responsible...'. Spatial specificity: No—no defined jurisdiction/coverage such as 'nationwide' or named regions. Weak language: Yes—repeated 'It is proposed'. Strong language: No—no mandates tied to hard numbers. Under the strict rule for individual policies, missing both timeline and quantifiable target makes it NON-SOUND.

policy_statement: Biennial M&E reporting by ministries on mitigation and adaptation actions, using standardized templates; CCCD collects and NCCC reviews every two years.
verbatim_text:
-  On a biennial basis, ministries will be responsible for reporting on the status of implementing the identified mitigation and adaptation actions and providing update for the new ones.
-  As is the case in the annual M&E report, the CCCD will be responsible for collecting the biennial M&E reports from the ministries.
extraction_rationale: Sets a recurring biennial reporting obligation and defines process/roles.
reasoning: This is a procedural MRV requirement (biennial reporting and data collection roles). It is auditable in the sense that reports can be checked for submission, and it includes a clear frequency (“biennial”). However, it does not specify any quantifiable climate outcome target (e.g., emissions reductions, number of actions implemented) or a deadline/year by which a specific outcome must be achieved. Under the strict rule for individual policies, missing a quantifiable target makes it NON-SOUND, and confidence must be capped.
validation_reasoning:
Quantifiable target: No. The text requires reporting but includes no measurable outcome metric (no % reductions, quantities, or implementation totals), only: "ministries will be responsible for reporting on the status of implementing the identified mitigation and adaptation actions".
Timeline: No. The text specifies a frequency—"On a biennial basis"—but does not give an explicit deadline or year (e.g., "by 2030").
Binding mechanism: No. It describes responsibilities ("will be responsible") but does not cite a law/mandate/ordinance/regulation establishing enforceability.
Spatial specificity: No. No jurisdictional scope (e.g., national, citywide, specific regions) is stated in the excerpt.
Weak language: No. It is not framed as "encourage" or "explore"; it is an obligation to report.
Strong language: No. While "will be responsible" indicates duty, there are no hard numeric performance requirements tied to outcomes.
Result: NON-SOUND because the strict gold-standard requirement fails (no quantifiable target and no explicit deadline/year). Confidence capped at 0.4 due to missing quantifiable target/timeline per rules.
